keyRules: Drones weighing ≥250 g (0.55 lb): mandatory registration with IDAC via Vuela Seguro (RAD-48)
Drones weighing more than 2 kg (4.4 lb): In addition to registration, a special IDAC permit is required
Minimum distance of 9 km (5 nautical miles) from airports and airfields
Flying is permitted only during the day—night flying requires a specific IDAC permit
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
No-fly zone: National Palace, Ministry of Defense, San Isidro Air Base
NO FLIGHT ZONE: Dominican Oil Refinery, Las Calderas Naval Base, military command posts
Commercial Operation: Remote Pilot Certificate + Additional Operational Permit
Do not interfere with air traffic during airport approach and departure patterns
importantNotes: 📋 LEGAL FRAMEWORK: Drone operations are regulated by Civil Aviation Law 491-06 + RAD-107 (small unmanned aircraft system) + RAD-48 (registration and labeling). The IDAC (Dominican Civil Aviation Institute) is the competent authority.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: These rules apply to non-residents. Drones weighing ≥250 g must be registered before operation. For recreational use of drones weighing ≤250 g (DJI Mini, Flip): registration is not required, but all other rules apply. Documentation is recommended upon entry into the country.

🏖️ PUNTA CANA AND COASTAL TOURIST AREAS: The airports PUJ (Punta Cana), SDQ (Las Américas), and STI (Santiago) have a 9-km exclusion zone. Saona, Catalina, Bayahibe, Las Galeras: Many beaches and resorts have additional restrictions—check with your hotel or tour operator before flying.

🌳 PROTECTED AREAS: Los Haitises, del Este (Saona), Sierra de Bahoruco, Jaragua, and Lago Enriquillo National Parks require a special permit from the Ministry of the Environment. Samaná Bay (whale watching, Jan–Mar): Additional restrictions are in place to avoid disturbing wildlife.

🏛️ PERMANENTLY RESTRICTED AREAS: National Palace (Santo Domingo), Ministry of Defense, all military bases (San Isidro, Las Calderas), and the Dominican Oil Refinery. The Colonial Zone (UNESCO World Heritage Site in Santo Domingo) has temporary restrictions—check beforehand.

primaryLinks.title: IDAC — Fly Safe (official drone portal)
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the Dominican Civil Aviation Institute for drone operators. Includes a comprehensive guide, RAD-107/48 regulations, registration requirements, and restricted areas

primaryLinks.title: Registration Requirements and Procedure
primaryLinks.description: Official list of required documents: drone technical specifications, photographs, pilot's certificate (if applicable), proof of liability insurance in accordance with RAD-107

primaryLinks.title: IDAC — sUAS/Drone Operations
primaryLinks.description: Official section on sUAS (Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems) operations. Special permits, authorization for commercial use, and controlled zones

primaryLinks.title: IDAC — Home Page
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the Dominican Civil Aviation Institute. Access to Law 491-06, the complete RAD regulations, and services for citizens

secondaryLinks.title: IDAC — DTAC (Department of Civil Aviation)
secondaryLinks.description: Official email address for registration and inquiries: DTAC@idac.gov.do. For operators of drones weighing more than 2 kg or for commercial use, direct contact is recommended.

secondaryLinks.title: Civil Aviation Law No. 491-06
secondaryLinks.description: General legal framework for all Dominican civil aviation, including unmanned aircraft. The basis upon which the RAD Regulations are established

secondaryLinks.title: Ministry of the Environment — Protected Areas
secondaryLinks.description: For protected areas such as Los Haitises National Park, Del Este National Park (Saona), and Samaná Bay: a specific permit is required before operating drones

fields.disclaimerText: Always check IDAC’s official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. The Dominican Republic enforces Civil Aviation Law 491-06 along with Regulations RAD-107 (sUAS operations) and RAD-48 (registration). The official website is Vuela Seguro (idac.gob.do/drone).
